plan: KeyTransform registers a KeyTransformExact whose process_rhs wraps each value per backend (JSON_QUOTE/JSON_EXTRACT on MySQL, JSON_QUERY/JSON_VALUE on Oracle, JSON_EXTRACT on SQLite), but there is no __in counterpart, so __in on a key transform falls back to generic lookups.In and compares raw params against JSON-extracted lhs values, matching nothing on non-native-JSON backends. Add a KeyTransformIn(lookups.In) in django/db/models/fields/json.py that overrides resolve_expression_parameter/process_rhs to apply the same backend-specific rhs wrapping as KeyTransformExact, and register it via KeyTransform.register_lookup(KeyTransformIn). The generic In lookup in lookups.py already exposes the resolve_expression_parameter hook, so no changes are needed outside json.py.
